sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Looking at the image, the player moved UP and is now at the frame location. The white marker is still visible above. The frame seems to have been collected - the charcoal frame is no longer visible at the right position. Let me check the segmentation to see if the frame was collected and what remains.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
green = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'N'][0]
gb = (min(p[0] for p in green['boundary']), min(p[1] for p in green['boundary']))
print(f"Green at: {gb}")

# Check remaining frames
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] in ('c', 'G', 'W') and n['pixels'] >= 12:
        b = (min(p[0] for p in n['boundary']), min(p[1] for p in n['boundary']))
        print(f"  {n['color']}: pixels={n['pixels']}, pos={b}, children={n['children']}")

# Check pink bar
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'P':
        print(f"Pink bar: {n['pixels']} pixels")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
